name: Ted Dunbar
abstract: Earl Theodore Dunbar (January 17, 1937 – May 29, 1998) was an American jazz guitarist, composer, and educator.

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: Jazz Stars at Midnight
Newport Jazz Festival: Benny Goodman Salute to Jazz Interactions
Music from Rutgers Presents University Composers, Program IV

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Foster
about_quotes.evidence: At Davidson College of Rutgers University I was one of the jazz professors under Larry Ridley, with Ted Dunbar, Kenny Barron, and the late Freddie Waits. We had a quintet called the Jazz Professors.
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Foster — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Barron
about_quotes.evidence: Then the next one is a duet, you and Ted Dunbar, In Tandem. ... That was actually – that wasn’t intended to be a record. That was a concert we did at Rutgers.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Barron — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: David N. Baker
about_quotes.evidence: We got to be really good buddies. Fact is, when – he would sit in with us almost every night. Then we started playing a lot of gigs together.
about_quotes.doc_title: David N. Baker — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Powell
about_quotes.evidence: I’ve got a tape that shows some of the finest work of Vernel Fournier, Jack Wilson, he’s a pianist in California, Ted Dunbar, Clifford Jordan, all these are on one album
about_quotes.doc_title: Benny Powell interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 16, 1999 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Noah Evans
about_quotes.evidence: And then I have a guitarist Ted Dunbar. He plays electric guitar. Oh he plays acoustic guitar too, but mostly electric and he teaches at Rutgers.

about_quotes.speaker: Larry Ridley
about_quotes.evidence: The first person I brought in was Ted Dunbar... I had four tenured professors in jazz. And four of them African-American.
about_quotes.doc_title: Larry Ridley interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 10, 2003
